sections.sourcing: No F&F-specific sourcing shifts this week, but new UK trade data is directionally important: UK apparel imports rose to US$4.89bn in Q1 2026 (+3.8% YoY), with Bangladesh the strongest performer (+7.3%, consolidating its lead as the UK's top supplier) and India up 13.4%, while Turkey and Pakistan declined. Crucially for Tesco's supply base, Bangladesh's DCTS duty-free access into the UK is confirmed to continue beyond its LDC graduation on 24 November 2026, so no duty disruption is expected for UK buyers in 2026-27.
sections.strategy: Quiet on new announcements this week. Ongoing context: the May 2025 online relaunch (2,200+ SKUs) continues to drive growth, and Tesco's Q1 2026/27 statement (18 June, 13 weeks to 30 May) showed UK LFL sales up 1.8% with non-food/online among the callouts, though no standalone F&F figure was broken out. Competitive backdrop is heating up: Asda is accelerating standalone George concept stores (10 in 2026, ambition of 50-100 stores and a £4bn+ brand), and Primark's click-and-collect plus Shein/Temu keep pressure on the value segment.
sections.pricing: F&F is running an aggressive up-to-50%-off summer clothing sale in stores through July 2026, alongside recurring 25%-off Clubcard clothing events. On the wider price battleground, Asda retained The Grocer 33 pricing award (cheapest in 29 of 50 weeks, July 2025-July 2026), beating Tesco and Sainsbury's on both base and loyalty pricing — keeping margin pressure on supermarket fashion price points.
sections.launches: First autumn 2026 pieces are landing now: 100+ autumn womenswear styles online and in stores, leaning into bold prints and autumnal cream/khaki/burgundy/chocolate tones — knitwear, 'expensive-looking' coats, waterproof raincoats, fleece-lined jackets and shirt dresses. An autumn/winter campaign from BBH is expected to follow the summer 'Style It Out' platform (second year, shot by Julia & Vincent), which is still running.
sections.gossip: F&F co-presented the 35th Graduate Fashion Week (15-18 June, Truman Brewery) with student design and marketing briefs, an F&F Digital Innovation Lab and a curated catwalk — a talent-pipeline and brand-elevation play trade press reads as part of F&F's push upmarket. Trade commentary continues to frame supermarket fashion (F&F, George, TU) as 'stepping up' against Zara/Mango-tier high street.
soWhat: Bangladesh's post-LDC DCTS duty-free continuity removes a key 2026-27 uncertainty — expect Tesco to keep consolidating volume there (UK imports from Bangladesh +7.3% in Q1), while Turkey's declining UK share suggests Turkish capacity may need sharper pricing to hold F&F orders.

Autumn 2026 range hitting stores now means AW26 production is largely shipped and SS27 development is live — pitch fabric/trim innovation in the autumn palette (rich browns, burgundy, khaki) and outerwear/knit categories where F&F is visibly investing.

Deep July discounting (up to 50% off) plus Asda's price-leadership run signals sustained cost pressure on buyers — expect tough price negotiations on repeat orders even as F&F pushes design-led, higher-spec product, which favours suppliers who can deliver elevated quality at value cost.
